incorrects.description: Student selected 6 — only counted Cara's books, did not add Ben's

steps.text: You found Cara's 6 books. The question asks for Ben and Cara in all—we need to add both amounts together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 9 — likely added Ben (5) and Dan (3) instead of Ben and Cara, or miscounted

steps.text: Let's look at the right people. We need Ben and Cara—check which bars those are, then add the two numbers.

incorrects.description: Student selected 14 — likely added Ava (8) and Cara (6) instead of Ben and Cara

steps.text: Check the names carefully. The question asks about Ben and Cara—find their bars, read the numbers, then add.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly — system models the correct answer

params.description: Ben and Cara bars highlight. Ben's bar labeled 5, Cara's bar labeled 6. Addition equation 5 + 6 = 11 appears.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. First, find Ben's bar—it shows 5 books. Then find Cara's bar—it shows 6 books. To find how many in all, we add: 5 plus 6 equals 11 books.
